articles.title: Trump Extends Ceasefire but Iran Threatens War Over Blockade | Hormuz Crisis Deepens | Latest N18G
articles.description: Tensions escalate sharply as Donald Trump issues a firm warning: there can be no deal if the Strait of Hormuz reopens. The statement signals a hardening stance by Washington even as diplomatic efforts continue in the background.The Strait of Hormuz is one of the world’s most critical energy routes, with a significant share of global oil shipments passing through it. Any decision to maintain or lift a blockade has far-reaching implications—not just for Iran and the U.S., but for global markets and energy security.Trump’s remarks come amid ongoing truce talks, rising military pressure, and increasing uncertainty over whether diplomacy can succeed. Analysts warn that tying a deal to Hormuz control could complicate negotiations further and push both sides toward confrontation. articles.title: Virginia Election Results: Voters Pass New Redistricting Congressional Map, Boost Dems For 2026
articles.description: Voters in Virginia approved a new congressional map that could shift the state’s delegation from a 6-5 split to a possible 10-1 Democratic advantage. The move is part of a broader redistricting battle and could have major implications for control of Congress in the 2026 midterms.

articles.title: Haryana Govt Reshuffles Over 40 Police Officers to Boost Efficiency
articles.description: The Haryana government has transferred over 40 Haryana Police Service (HPS) officers, including DSPs and ACPs, across key districts like Gurugram and Faridabad. The move aims to boost law enforcement and administrative efficiency across the state.

articles.title: 'Won't Stop Fighting': Houthi Chief Says War Not Over Despite Trump Ceasefire Extension
articles.description: The guns are silent, but the blockade remains. President Donald Trump has officially extended the ceasefire with Iran, but there’s a catch. Brokered at the urgent request of Pakistan’s Field Marshal Asim Munir and PM Shehbaz Sharif, this extension is a final window for Tehran to present a unified proposal. With Houthi leaders already promising that "more fighting is coming" and the U.S. Navy maintaining its grip on the Strait of Hormuz, the region is holding its breath. Is this the path to peace or just the calm before the storm?

The election, conducted across 274 polling stations, remained peaceful, marking a significant milestone in Mizoram’s urban local governance. articles.title: Second maternity leave can't be denied merely because sought within 2 years of first: Allahabad High Court
articles.description: Allahabad High Court: In a key 2026 ruling, the court said second maternity leave cannot be denied within two years of the first. It held the Maternity Benefit Act overrides state rules, directing leave approval and reinforcing women’s legal rights at work.

articles.title: US-Iran war: JD Vance's Pakistan trip for peace talks on hold
articles.description: Iran war news: US Vice President JD Vance’s planned Pakistan visit for Iran talks is on hold. Iran hasn’t responded to US proposals. With the ceasefire nearing expiry, uncertainty looms. Pakistan has urged both sides to extend the truce and resume dialogue.

articles.title: West Bengal elections: Junglemahal’s 25 seats turn battleground as BJP targets TMC’s development pitch
articles.description: Purulia, Bankura, and Jhargram, the gateway to West Bengal's Junglemahal, are witnessing a fierce political battle. While Trinamool banks on welfare schemes and development claims, BJP challenges with promises of industrial growth and job creation, targeting voter dissatisfaction with the current rule.
